---
title: "Curriculum Vitae"
---

<p><a class="cv-download-button" href="files/cv-mingyuan-you.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Download PDF</a></p>

## Mingyuan You (Billy)

Tel. +44 7513 607740 | [billyyou135y@gmail.com](mailto:billyyou135y@gmail.com) | [mingyuan.you.25@ucl.ac.uk](mailto:mingyuan.you.25@ucl.ac.uk) | [LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/in/mingyuan-you)

## Profile

An aspiring built environment student motivated to pursue chartered RTPI membership within a multidisciplinary planning context. Strong working understanding of the UK's devolved planning systems, local authority processes and planning legislation. A diligent and proactive learner with experience collaborating across policy, development management and infrastructure-related workstreams. Particularly interested in sustainable housing delivery and retrofitting and protecting the historic built environment.

Currently studying an MSc in Housing and City Planning at University College London (UCL). Graduated with a 2:1 in BSc Urban Planning and Development (Sandwich Year) from Cardiff University.

## Relevant Work Experience

### Anyang Zhongshen Real Estate - Kampala, Uganda

**07/2025 - 09/2025**

- Market research: Assisted with a market feasibility report for a venue rental site on the Entebbe-Kampala highway. Conducted site visits to existing venues in the Kampala metropolitan area and liaised with site management teams to gather rental data.

### Planning Intern, Kampala Capital City Authority (KCCA) - Kampala, Uganda

**08/2024 - 09/2024**

- Planning enforcement: Investigated possible breaches of planning development around the Greater Kampala Metropolitan Area.
- Community Resolution Team: Conducted community mediation between communities and other stakeholders.

### Student Planner, The City of Edinburgh Council (CEC) - Edinburgh, UK

**06/2023 - 08/2024**

- Heritage and householders: Assessed and determined planning and related applications, including listed building consents, advertisement consents and certificates of lawfulness.
- Consultations: Engaged with Council services, key agencies, community councils, amenity bodies and external organisations for input into planning and related applications.
- Development planning: Assisted with obtaining, analysing and interpreting information to inform the preparation of CEC's current local development plan, City Plan 2030.

## Education

### MSc Housing and City Planning, UCL - London, UK

**09/2025 - 09/2026**

- Relevant modules: Management of Housing Development Projects; Planning for Housing: Process; Sustainable Residential Design & Retrofit; Planning for Housing: Project; Comparative Planning; Real Estate & Infrastructure Law; The Economics & Finance of Housing Development.
- RICS and specialist RTPI accredited.

### BSc Urban Planning & Development (PPY), Cardiff University - Cardiff, UK

**09/2021 - 07/2025 | 2:1**

- Relevant modules: Community Engagement, Mediation & Negotiation Skills (84%); Planning Theory & Practice (74%); Site Planning & Development (70%); Exploring Contemporary Issues in Geography & Planning (72%); Digital Planning (70%); Society, Diversity & Planning (68%); Key Issues in Urban Planning (66%).
- RICS and RTPI accredited.
- Dissertation (68%) on Historic Preservation Amid the Climate Emergency in Edinburgh.
- ISEP (formerly IEMA) Associate Membership Environmental Certification.

### A level, Ellesmere College - Ellesmere, UK

**09/2019 - 06/2021**

- Subjects: Business Studies (B), History (B), Physics (C), ESB Merit.
- Leadership role: Assistant Boarding-House Captain.

### IGCSE, St Andrews Turi Molo - Molo, Kenya

**09/2016 - 06/2019**

- Subjects: 10 IGCSEs, grades A*-B. Mathematics A, English Language B.

## Key Skills

### Decision Making

- Assessed 140 planning applications at CEC through delegated planning decisions, requiring policy understanding and interpretation across a range of development types with support from experienced team members.
- Applied existing and incoming retail and environmental policy for internal consultation requests from other planning officers.

### Effective Collaboration

- Co-coordinated the 2024 Edinburgh Planning Committee Sustainability and Heritage Tour, developing the Planning Committee's relationship with development architects Bennetts Associates, the University of Edinburgh Estates Department and Montagu Evans.

### Research and Analytical

- For Anyang Zhongshen Real Estate, cold-called venue teams to gather rental data and conducted in-person site visits with colleagues to identify unique venue traits.

### Teamwork and Leadership

- Conducted qualitative and quantitative research with other students to investigate mobility and commuting methods in Cardiff, supporting deadlines and continuous improvement among teammates.
- Prepared sessions and continuously supported new colleagues to use planning software such as IDOX.

### Planning and Organising

- Independently tracked appeals from the DPEA and Local Review Body to identify trends in policy interpretation and unique decisions. Organised and summarised findings for quarterly policy working group meetings with planning officers.

### Computer Literate

- Competent in Microsoft Office applications, SketchUp, Digi Maps, Uniform and IDOX case management software.

## Other Work Experience

### Ambassador, School of Geography & Planning Cardiff University - Cardiff, UK

**06/2025 - 06/2025**

- Student Ambassador: Met prospective geography and planning students during Cardiff University open days, assisting with Q&As and providing personal reflections on the undergraduate degree and placement year.

### GeoPlan Society Committee, Cardiff University - Cardiff, UK

**10/2022 - 06/2023**

- Committee Treasurer: Communicated with Cardiff University society managers to handle payments, procurement and compliance forms.
- Events organiser: Delivered an end-of-year dinner for approximately 220 attendees.

### Assistant Team Leader, Brentford FC CST/NCS - London, UK

**06/2022 - 07/2022**

- Supervisor: Responsible for pastoral care and safety needs of 20 young people.
- Activities Organiser: Supported and encouraged students on classroom-based tasks and outdoor volunteering activities.

## Interests and Volunteering

- Fundraising: 2024 Edinburgh Marathon, raised £650 plus £116 Gift Aid for MacMillan Cancer Support.
- Fundraising: 2022 Cardiff University Cardiff Half Marathon, raised £160 for Cardiff University cancer research.
- Sports: Weekly Parkrun and occasional Parkrun volunteer.

## Other Competencies

- Full UK driving license.
